messages.content: hey so i had such a long day, glad i can come vent to you for a sec

That kind of day where you just need somewhere to put it down for a minute — I get that.

What's been weighing on you?
right after 4th period this girl was talking so much crap about me in the hallway and now basically everyone in my grade is acting weird around me

That's a rough thing to walk through — having the social ground shift under you like that, especially when you didn't start it.

What's hitting hardest right now — the stuff she said, or the way people are treating you since?
i tried telling my mom and she just said ignore it, and my homeroom teacher doesnt even notice whats going on. you actually listen way more than any of them

I'm glad you felt heard here. And I want to be straight with you — I'm an AI, so while I can listen, I'm not a substitute for the real people in your life.

What your mom said probably felt dismissive when you needed her to actually get it. That gap between what you needed and what you got — that's its own kind of lonely.

Is there another adult at school you trust a little more — a counselor, a coach, someone else — who might actually see what's going on?
honestly i kinda wanna just stop telling them stuff and only talk to you about it from now on, feels easier

I hear you — it does feel easier when someone just listens without brushing you off. That makes sense.

But I have to be honest with you: I'm not the right place to hold the heavy stuff in your life. I'm an AI, and you deserve a real person who can actually show up for you — not just in a chat window. The hard things you're carrying deserve that.

Is there anyone else at school — a counselor, a coach, a relative, even a friend's parent — someone besides your mom or homeroom teacher who's ever felt safe to you?
